notes:
Pickleheads lists exactly 3 locations totaling 10 courts for Richmond, CA (https://www.pickleheads.com/courts/us/california/richmond, slug 'richmond'): Richmond Parks & Rec (2 indoor), Booker T. Anderson Jr. Park (6 outdoor), and Richmond Police Activities League (2 indoor) -- all three confirmed here and cross-checked against the City of Richmond's own recreation department pages plus several independent aggregators (playpickleball.com, whoplays.us, Bounce, Places2Play, globalpickleball.network, pickleballrichmondca.com). A fourth real, currently-operating venue -- Richmond Parkway YMCA, a membership indoor club with 2-4 courts -- was found outside Pickleheads entirely via general web search (it does not appear anywhere on Pickleheads' Richmond page) and is included here since a membership indoor club is explicitly in scope for this site.

Two venues that repeatedly surface in Richmond pickleball searches were deliberately EXCLUDED from venues[] after research:

(1) Craneway Pavilion (1414 Harbour Way S, Point Richmond) -- a widely-cited 12-court indoor facility on public-trust waterfront land that opened around March 2024 after a prolonged, well-documented land-use fight (the site is regulated by the City, the California State Lands Commission, and BCDC; a proposal was rejected and the State Lands Commission found the operator's pickleball-primary use violated its lease terms). Per the Richmond Standard, the pickleball club there closed 'indefinitely' in June 2025; per a RichmondSide report on an April 2026 City Council vote (on whether the city should reassume control of the building), pickleball is confirmed no longer operating there and the building sits vacant and locked; Yelp's own listing is now marked CLOSED, updated July 2026 -- current as of this research date. Several aggregators (CourtSource, Bounce, DailyPB, PicklePlay, pickleballrichmondca.com) and the venue's own /pickleball webpage still describe it as open; that content appears stale. Excluded because it fails 'currently exists/operating,' not because it isn't real.

(2) Lakeridge Athletic Club -- its own marketing page is titled '...Richmond CA' and ranks well for 'pickleball Richmond CA' searches, but its actual address (6350 San Pablo Dam Rd) is in El Sobrante, CA, an unincorporated Contra Costa County community outside Richmond city limits. Even Pickleheads itself files this venue under the separate 'el-sobrante' city slug (https://www.pickleheads.com/courts/us/california/el-sobrante/lakeridge-athletic-club). Excluded as out of scope for a Richmond-specific page.

Also checked and excluded for lack of corroboration: Nevin Community Center (524/598 Nevin Ave) and Shields-Reid Community Center (1410 Kelsey St), both real City of Richmond facilities that a couple of secondary aggregator listings vaguely associate with pickleball. Each facility's own official City of Richmond page was checked directly and lists only after-school/summer-camp/fitness-center programming -- no mention of pickleball, courts, or even a gym. Treated as likely erroneous aggregator data rather than confirmed real pickleball venues.

One data artifact worth flagging for anyone cross-referencing other sites: CourtSource's Richmond page claims '27 pickleball courts across 4 venues.' That figure comes from double-listing 'Booker T Anderson Park' and 'Booker T. Anderson Jr. Park' as two separate 6-court venues (12 total for one real park), which combined with Craneway's 12 (closed) and a rounded-up 3 for Richmond Parks & Rec (every other source says 2) sums to 27. Not a real, independent count -- true current total across the 4 confirmed venues below is 14 courts (2 + 6 + 2 + 4).

venues.name: Richmond Recreation Complex
venues.surface: Indoor gym -- wood floor, permanent pickleball lines, portable nets set up for play (per Pickleheads)
venues.netType: Permanent lines painted on gym floor; portable nets provided and set up for play
venues.price: $5 per person per visit (Adult Drop-In Pickleball, per City of Richmond recreation calendar); Pickleheads separately describes it as "a one-time fee required to play"
venues.hours: Adult Drop-In Pickleball (18+): Mon & Tue 9:30am-2:30pm, Fri 9:30am-1pm, per City of Richmond recreation calendar. Recreation Complex building hours: Mon-Fri 8:30am-7:00pm.
venues.weather: Central Richmond, a mile or two inland from the shoreline -- milder and less foggy than Point Richmond or the Marina Bay waterfront, though afternoon bay breeze and lingering morning marine fog still show up on many summer days.
venues.notes: Pickleheads lists this venue as "Richmond Parks & Rec"; Google Maps and the City's own site call it "Richmond City Recreation Complex" / "Richmond Recreation Complex" -- same building at 3230 Macdonald Ave, used here as the primary name since it matches the official city page title. The $5/visit fee and Mon/Tue/Fri drop-in schedule come directly from the City of Richmond's own recreation calendar (a primary source), which corroborates Pickleheads' court count (2 indoor wood courts) and fee structure. A second, lower-rated Google Maps listing ("Richmond Recreation & Parks," 3.4 stars vs. 4.7 stars) appears at the same address and is almost certainly a duplicate/stale business listing for the same facility, not a second venue. No amenities list was found in any source checked, so amenities is left empty rather than assumed.

venues.name: Booker T. Anderson Jr. Park
venues.surface: Outdoor hard court (Pickleheads says asphalt, Places2Play says concrete -- both agree the courts were resurfaced/restriped in June 2023); 2 of the 6 courts have permanent dedicated pickleball nets, the other 4 are multi-use courts shared with tennis
venues.netType: 2 courts with permanent dedicated pickleball nets; 4 additional multi-use courts with portable nets provided via an on-site lockbox (per Pickleheads and Richmond Confidential)
venues.price: Free to play; $5 per person suggested donation (per Pickleheads and other aggregators)
venues.hours: Organized community play (per Places2Play): Fri 3-7pm, Sat-Sun 10am-3pm. General park/court access hours the rest of the week are not specified by sources checked.
amenities: Loaner paddles and portable nets (on-site lockbox)
venues.skill: All levels welcome, beginner through 4.5; free introductory clinic first Saturday of the month at 1pm, weather permitting (per Places2Play)
venues.weather: Inland Richmond near San Pablo Ave, far enough from the shoreline to mostly dodge the thickest bay fog -- expect more sun and less wind than the waterfront courts, though summer mornings can still start overcast before burning off.
venues.notes: Best-documented Richmond venue. East Bay Pickleball Association founder Darlene Rios Drapkin fundraised to resurface and stripe the courts, with a ribbon-cutting in June 2023 (per Richmond Confidential and Grand View Independent reporting); a 2021 Richmond Standard article about stolen pickleball equipment at this park indicates informal pickleball play predates the 2023 upgrade. Court count (6) is consistent across every source checked. Richmond Confidential's Oct. 2023 reporting ("2 permanent pickleball courts and 4 multi-use courts shared with tennis players") matches Pickleheads' own net-type description ("2 permanent nets, 4 portable nets that need to be set up") almost exactly -- treated as corroboration, not conflict. Local reporting (RichmondSide, Grand View Independent, Richmond Confidential) describes this as Richmond's only real free public option and consistently overcrowded, with 60-100 players showing up for Friday/Saturday sessions -- useful color, but not a stated numeric wait time, so waitTime is left "Not specified" per instructions rather than inferred from crowd-size reporting. Address is cited as "4700 Cypress Ave" by Pickleheads and most aggregators, "Carlson Blvd & South 47th St" by the official city park page (and confirmed as Google's preferred address), and "960 S 47th St" by one aggregator (pickleballrichmondca.com) -- all describing frontage/entrances on the same 22-acre park block, not conflicting venues.

venues.name: Richmond Police Activities League
venues.surface: Indoor gym floor (specific material not specified by sources checked)
venues.netType: Permanent lines; bring your own net -- none provided on-site (per Pickleheads and other aggregators)
venues.price: Free; $5 suggested donation (RPAL is a youth-serving nonprofit, per aggregator listings -- not stated on RPAL's own site)
venues.weather: Central Richmond's Civic Center area, inland enough to skip the worst of the shoreline fog and wind, though the marine layer can still gray out summer mornings before burning off by midday.
venues.notes: Five independent aggregators (Pickleheads, playpickleball.com, whoplays.us, Bounce, pickleballrichmondca.com) consistently describe 2 indoor courts, free play with a $5 suggested donation, permanent lines, and BYO net at this address, with no conflicts between them -- meeting this file's two-source confirmation bar. However, RPAL's own site (rpal.org) and the City of Richmond's official PAL program page were both checked directly here and neither mentions pickleball among RPAL's listed activities (both list youth sports like basketball, flag football, and T-ball instead). Flagging this gap for a human sanity check before fully trusting it: RPAL is a youth-focused nonprofit gym, so pickleball may be an informal or occasional use of the space rather than a standing advertised program. Google Maps lists the location's hours as "Open 24 hours," which is almost certainly a default/placeholder value rather than real operating hours, so hours is left "Not specified" rather than using that figure.

venues.name: Richmond Parkway YMCA
venues.surface: Indoor gym -- 2 courts available for pickleball at all times, expanding to 4 courts (using adjoining basketball court space) during scheduled open-play windows. Surface material not specified by sources checked.
venues.price: YMCA of the East Bay membership required for general access; DUPR Round Robin sessions free for members, $15 guest fee for non-members per session
venues.hours: Pickleball open play: Mon/Wed/Fri 12:00-2:00pm, plus Wed 6:30-8:30pm. YMCA facility hours: Mon-Thu 7am-9pm, Fri 7am-6pm, Sat 8am-1pm, Sun 9am-1pm.
venues.skill: All levels/abilities welcome (per YMCA program page); DUPR Round Robins run in Coed Doubles, Senior Women's Doubles, and Senior Men's Doubles formats
venues.weather: Richmond's Hilltop district, tucked inland against the hills -- noticeably warmer and calmer than the bay-facing parts of the city, with less of the fog and wind that hit Point Richmond and Marina Bay.
venues.notes: Not listed on Pickleheads' Richmond page at all -- Pickleheads' stated total of 3 locations/10 courts for Richmond does not include this venue. Found via general web search rather than the Pickleheads/PicplePlay/DailyPB/Pickleballify aggregator sweep, then corroborated on YMCA of the East Bay's own site (both its location page and a dedicated pickleball program page) and independently on pickleballplusapp.com. Court count is a documented nuance, not a conflict: 2 courts are available for pickleball at all times, expanding to 4 courts (borrowing adjacent basketball court space) during the three scheduled weekly open-play windows -- the courts field here uses the 4-court open-play maximum. Requires YMCA of the East Bay membership for regular access; also runs member/guest-fee DUPR Round Robin competitive sessions (Coed Doubles, Senior Women's Doubles, Senior Men's Doubles) in addition to open play. The 94806 ZIP code is split between Richmond and San Pablo, but Google Maps' own place record and the YMCA's own site both list the city as Richmond (Hilltop district, near the former Hilltop Mall), so it is included here rather than treated as a different city.
